twitter_thread: 73% of side hustles fail within 2 years, with $41k in average debt. Poor financial planning is the main culprit.
Allocate 50% of side hustle income to taxes, 30% to savings, and 20% to expenses. This rule saved me $10k last year.
Commingling funds cost one side hustler $12k in tax penalties. Open a separate business bank account with Capital One, which has a $0 monthly fee.
Tracking expenses with QuickBooks saved another side hustler $5k in tax deductions. Wave is another solid option.
A solo 401(k) can save side hustlers $5,500 in annual retirement contributions. Don't overlook this benefit.
What's the most costly side hustle financial mistake you've made or seen? #sidehustle #financetips
pack.linkedin: 73% of side hustles fail within two years, often due to poor financial planning. To avoid this, allocate 50% of income to taxes, 30% to savings, and 20% to expenses. A separate business bank account with a low-fee bank like Capital One is crucial, as is tracking expenses with a tool like QuickBooks or Wave. A solo 401(k) can also save $5,500 in annual retirement contributions. What specific financial strategies have you used to succeed in your side hustle?
tiktok_hooks: Side hustles are tanking due to one simple financial mistake
The $12k error that'll kill your side hustle
Why a separate bank account is non-negotiable for side hustlers
pack.reddit_headline: 73% of side hustles fail within 2 years: can better financial planning turn the tide?
